spec_match.rationale: Listens on `beforeinput` and `input` on a contenteditable, inspects `inputType` and `dataTransfer`, and calls `preventDefault()` exactly as the spec describes. The simulated insertions exercise the same code path Android IME media triggers.

interactive.rationale: Live event log + MIME-toggle switches + preventDefault checkbox + three simulated-insertion buttons make this a working monitor, not a code-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a live event log with MIME-acceptance toggles and a preventDefault override — all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Single-file insertions are covered, but no multi-file dataTransfer (IMEs may commit several stickers at once), no `dataTransfer.getData('text/plain')` text-fallback case, and no demonstration of what happens on a non-editable target.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872 link anywhere on the page — the self-heal anchor is missing.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables and overrides button colour with background, but the page leans on unicode glyphs (`✓`, `✕`, etc.) for log markers; convention is inline SVG with currentColor.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also has no chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872 anchor. Add it so the slug-recovery script can find the ID.

openQuestions.title: Demo multi-file insertion
openQuestions.detail: Android IMEs can commit several items in a single InputContentInfo (e.g. a sticker pack). The dataTransfer.items list will have length > 1. Add a `simulate sticker pack` button that fires three image items in one event so the visitor sees the loop, not just `items[0]`.

openQuestions.title: Replace unicode glyph markers with inline SVG
openQuestions.detail: Log rows currently use `✓` / `✕` to indicate accept/reject. Project convention is inline SVG icons. Replace each glyph with a small SVG so colour theming works.

openQuestions.title: Show the text-fallback dataTransfer.getData path
openQuestions.detail: When the IME commits a sticker, the spec lets it set a textual fallback alongside the image. Surface `dataTransfer.getData('text/plain')` in the event readout so the visitor sees the alt-text contract too.

openQuestions.title: Show the same flow on a non-contenteditable target
openQuestions.detail: If the focused element isn't editable, the event fires but `beforeinput` has no `dataTransfer`. Demoing that delta clarifies why the contenteditable surface matters.

summary: Solid event-log probe with real listeners, MIME toggles, and preventDefault control. Missing the chromestatus reference link and limited to single-item dataTransfers — both fixable in a small follow-up.
